architecture: GRU(hidden=48) over last K=8 quarters of 34 features, masked last-step readout, isotonic-calibrated
verdict: The GRU sequence challenger scores OOT PR-AUC 0.207 vs the served GBM 0.301 (delta -0.094). The GRU point estimate is LOWER, but it falls INSIDE the served model bootstrap PR-AUC interval [0.191, 0.438], and at 66 OOT failures the paired test has ~6% power (G0), so the two are NOT statistically separable in either direction. Note the large inner-validation PR-AUC (0.607) collapsing to 0.207 out-of-time: the trajectory signal the GRU learns in-sample does not transfer across the regime/cohort shift, consistent with the failure-type decomposition. The trajectory architecture is the theoretically-matched design for within-bank autocorrelation, but it does not beat the incumbent here. It is reported as a challenger, not a replacement; the GBM remains served on grounds of point estimate, calibration, monotonicity, and interpretability.
